American Bankers Association ABA routing number for transfers.
Access Customer Name Abbreviation. For CABS and SECABS invoices, the field may be populated with XX for a multiple State account.
Accounts Payable Code. In BillTrak, it is assigned to a BAN for tracking expenses.
A and Z location and pulsing.
Access Service Group. Field in the Access Service Request that identifies the specific End Office or Tandem CLLI code assigned to a particular circuit or group of circuits.
Call Detail Record. A log of network events like calls or data usage.
Connecting Facility Assignment. The carrier system and channel that is used for the high capacity interface on access service.
Carrier Identification Code assigned to a customer when a single CIC is present; only applies the switched invoices.
Competitive Local Exchange Carrier. A telecom company that competes with ILECs after the 1996 Telecommunications Act opened up markets for competition. They may lease infrastructure from ILECs.

Common Language Location Identifier. An 11-character, globally unique code that identifies a physical location, network site, or equipment in the telecommunications industry. It serves as a universal identifier for specific physical points in a network.
Digital Signal 1, a standard for transmitting data at 1.544 megabits per second Mbps.
Electronic Data Interchange. An exchange of standardized business documents, such as invoices, between companies. EDI 811 is a common invoice format.
General Ledger Code. A unique number or code used in accounting to classify and track financial transactions.
The BAN associated with the high capacity circuit.

Code Inter-exchange Customer Service code. The four-digit code that identifies the location where access customer bill inquiries are processed. Dispute BAN When the consolidated dispute processing option is enabled, you have the option to link the account to a Dispute BAN. This field identifies the BAN to which disputes shall be filed.
Interexchange Customer Service Center. 4-character alphanumeric identifier e.g., FL02, NC01 that identifies a specific service center.
Incumbent Local Exchange Carrier. One of the original, often monopolistic, landline phone companies that existed prior to 1996 Telecommunications Act e.g., AT&T.
Local Access Transport Area. The 3-digit code denoting the LATA for which billing charges are incurred. For CABS and SECABS invoices, the field may be populated with zeroes for a multi-LATA account.
Local Exchange Routing Guide. Comprehensive database used primarily for call routing, but it also contains data essential for determining the correct taxes and regulatory fees.
IntraLATA Pre-subscription Indicator Code. The carrier the customer selected for IntraLATA traffic.
Local Transport. The LTP field indicates the type of Local Transport.
Originating Company Code. A unique, four-digit code identifying the vendor. National Exchange Carrier Association NECA maintains the list of valid values.
Pre-subscription Indicator Code PIC of the carrier the customer selected for InterLATA traffic.
Two Six Code. The unique identifier assigned to the message trunk group. There can be multiple TSCs associated with a single ASG/OCL.
Universal Service Order Code. A code identifying the specific item of equipment or service provided.
Voice over Internet Protocol. Service that allows you to make phone calls over your internet connection.
